summary: Iteration 4 SCOUT security features fully verified. Backend: 15/15 new tests + 40/40 iter3 regression all PASS. Frontend: SecuritySettings page loads with TopBar, BÁSICO/PROTECTED banner toggles correctly, all 3 security rows present, Change-Password modal flows (mismatch + wrong-current errors visible), 2FA setup modal renders QR + 32-char secret + 6-digit input + wrong-code error, 2FA verify enables and the row updates to 'Ativada · Google Authenticator', PROTECTED banner appears, Disable 2FA modal opens with code input + submit, Trusted Devices list shows multiple sessions with 'ATUAL' badge on current and revoke buttons. Biometric row correctly shows 'Não suportado neste dispositivo' in headless. Found and fixed a real backend bug in WebAuthn endpoints (PublicKeyCredentialCreationOptions.model_dump_json doesn't exist on webauthn 2.7.1) - replaced with webauthn.helpers.options_to_json and removed deprecated *.parse_raw calls in verify endpoints (passing dict directly to verify_registration/authentication_response is supported in 2.x).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now ~1326 lines - splitting into routers (auth, security, events, rewards, admin) is overdue for maintainability
Disable2FAModal uses native window.alert/window.confirm; consider replacing with in-app toast/sheet (currently blocks Playwright assertions)

Security: password change does NOT invalidate other sessions/devices - after a password rotation, other devices keep working with their JWTs. Consider rotating JWT secret per-user or adding a password_changed_at claim and rejecting older tokens.
POST /auth/change-password accepts new_password identical to current_password (no check). Cheap to add for safety.
/auth/biometric/disable wipes all credentials with no confirmation token (relies on cookie auth only). For high-value op, consider requiring current password.
/auth/sessions list does not paginate (to_list(200)). Fine now, but flag for scale.

Trusted-device records have no expiry/cleanup; revoking a device only removes its DB row - the JWT itself is still valid until expiry (7 days). The server does not check device existence on each request, so a revoked device could still hit /api/auth/me. Recommend adding device existence check in get_current_user.

WebAuthn verify endpoints: previously used pydantic v1 .parse_raw on RegistrationCredential/AuthenticationCredential which don't expose those methods in webauthn 2.7.1 - FIXED by passing dict to verify_*_response (which supports it natively in v2).
/auth/biometric/auth/options leaks user existence (404 vs 200). Consider returning generic options to avoid user enumeration.
updated_files: /app/backend/server.py (fixed: WebAuthn options_to_json + removed parse_raw calls in verify endpoints)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter4 security passes. Note: The WebAuthn library is webauthn==2.7.1 - DO NOT use *.parse_raw(...) or .model_dump_json(...) on its credential structs (those are pydantic v1 idioms removed in v2). Use webauthn.helpers.options_to_json(options) for output, and pass dict directly to verify_registration_response/verify_authentication_response (supported via Union[str, Dict, ...] type). data-testid map: security-settings, sec-change-password, sec-2fa, sec-bio, change-password-modal, cp-current/cp-new/cp-confirm/cp-show/cp-submit, disable-2fa-modal, d2fa-code/d2fa-submit, qr-image, 2fa-secret, 2fa-code, 2fa-verify, 2fa-done, modal-close, session-{device_id}, revoke-{device_id}. To compute TOTP code in Python: pyotp.TOTP(secret).now(). To reset 2FA on a user during testing: db.users.updateOne({email:'X'}, {$set:{two_factor_enabled:false}, $unset:{two_factor_secret:'', two_factor_secret_pending:''}}). Disable2FAModal currently uses window.alert which blocks Playwright tests after success - override window.alert and window.confirm in tests to proceed.
rca of the issue: Bug RCA: /auth/biometric/register/options returned 500 because the webauthn 2.7.1 library returns an instance of PublicKeyCredentialCreationOptions which is a regular dataclass (not pydantic), so calling options.model_dump_json() raises AttributeError. Fix: use webauthn.helpers.options_to_json(options) then json.loads to convert to a dict for FastAPI serialization. Same fix applied to biometric/auth/options. Also pre-emptively fixed the verify endpoints which used the deprecated RegistrationCredential.parse_raw / AuthenticationCredential.parse_raw idioms - in webauthn 2.x verify_registration_response and verify_authentication_response accept Union[str, Dict, struct] natively, so we pass the request body dict directly. This bug would have manifested only when a real browser attempted WebAuthn registration; biometric headless tests verify the options endpoint shape; verify endpoints can only be exercised by a real authenticator.
